content.text:
<think>
Looking at this rank chart showing "Top 20 countries of origin for travellers coming to Canada," I need to find the largest rank change over any single year period.

The China line (red) is highlighted because it's the most dramatic story:
- 1990: rank 19
- Steadily rises through the years
- ~2017: rank 2

Tracing the year-by-year segments:
- 1990→1991: ~19 to ~17 (change of ~2)
- Around 1999-2001: ~15 to ~12 (change of ~3)
- Around 2003-2005: ~13 to ~11 (change of ~2)
- Around 2013-2015: ~7 to ~4 (change of ~3)

China's biggest visible single-year jumps are approximately 2-3 ranks. The gray country lines do show some notable single-year movements, but they tend to oscillate back and forth (large jump up, then back down).

China's consistent upward trajectory — never reversing — combined with a total change of 17 ranks (from 19 to 2) over the chart period, and visible single-year jumps of 2-3 ranks in several periods, makes it the standout country for rank change. The chart is literally titled "The rise of the Chinese tourist," emphasizing China's dramatic rank improvement.
</think>

<answer>
China. Starting at rank 19 in 1990 and rising to rank 2 by ~2017, China showed the largest rank changes over single-year periods — with jumps of approximately 2–3 positions per year most visibly around 1999–2001 and 2013–2015. No other country on the chart shows a comparable sustained directional change; the gray country lines tend to oscillate up and down without the consistent upward momentum China demonstrated.
</answer>
